tool.name: Extract dataset
tool.description: from a collection by index or identifier

param.name: which_dataset
param.label: How should a dataset be selected?

tool.help:
======== Synopsis ========
Extracts a single dataset from a collection by position or identifier.
=========== Description ===========
This tool pulls one dataset out of a collection and places it in your history as a standalone dataset. You can select the dataset in three ways:
- **The first dataset** — takes the first element of the collection. - **Select by index** — takes the element at a specific position (starting from 0). - **Select by element identifier** — takes the element whose name matches the given identifier.
The extracted dataset is a copy of the original, so the input collection is not modified.
======== Examples ========
**Extract from a flat list** (case **A** in the image below)
Given a list collection with three elements::
List: "RNA-seq samples"
control → control.fastq treated → treated.fastq reference → reference.fastq
Selecting **by element identifier** = ``treated`` produces a single standalone dataset: ``treated.fastq``. Selecting **by index** = ``0`` would extract ``control.fastq`` instead.
-------
**Extract from a nested collection** (case **B** in the image below)
When the input is a nested collection such as ``list:list``, Galaxy automatically maps the extraction over the outer list. The same selection is applied to every inner list, and the result is a flat list — one nesting level is removed.
For example, given a ``list:list``::
list:list: "Paired analysis"
sampleA → [Dataset 1, Dataset 2, Dataset 3] sampleB → [Dataset A, Dataset B, Dataset C]
Extracting **by index** = ``1`` pulls element 1 from each inner list and produces a flat list::
List: "Paired analysis (extracted element)"
sampleA → Dataset 2 sampleB → Dataset B
The outer identifiers (``sampleA``, ``sampleB``) are preserved. The inner lists are replaced by the single extracted element from each.
-------
.. image:: ${static_path}/images/tools/collection_ops/extract_dataset.svg :alt: Extract dataset from collection :width: 800
-------
.. class:: warningmark
**Note**: Dataset index (numbering) begins with 0 (zero).
-----
.. class:: infomark
@QUOTA_USAGE_NOTE@
